title: Linux ssh-agent not working?
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman remote always asks for my ssh key.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman --log-level debug --connection server ps
2. asks for key
3. podman --log-level debug --connection server ps
4. asks for key

**Describe the results you received:**
Asks for key

**Describe the results you expected:**
Asks only once the key is requested on my system.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Happens always

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Git Commit:   ad42af94903ce4f3c3cd0693e4e17e4286bf094b-dirty
Built:        Wed Oct 19 23:09:30 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: @cdoern PTAL
is this on rc1 or on the final release? ssh was broken on rc1

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Git Commit:   ad42af94903ce4f3c3cd0693e4e17e4286bf094b-dirty
Built:        Wed Oct 19 23:09:30 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
@MartinX3 is it possible for you to remove and recreate the connection? I have a sneaking suspicion this will fix it.

I created it after installing 4.3.0 and already removed it to recreate it.
But I can try it again after I finish my job today.

> I created it after installing 4.3.0 and already removed it to recreate it. But I can try it again after I finish my job today.

In the meantime I will take a look into what could be going on. Any quirks of the system you are running on/creating the connection with I should know about? key type... weird OS... etc ?

Thank you

I use this weird Arch Linux everyone is talking about.
My key is `ed25519`.
I use `openssh 9.1p1` with its ssh-agent.

It works using `ssh` into a server or using `git`.

```bash
podman system connection remove  servername
podman system connection add servername --identity ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 ssh://servername@exploding-hamster.duckdns.org:43253/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
```

Same issue :(
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove stale
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove stale
@cdoern have you found anything?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove stale
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove stale

The issue appears to be happening here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/189b09d82e7c7892b8dba5c154e0210550b3fd55/vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/ssh/connection_golang.go#L224-L232
`SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is used only when `iden` is empty, so to make it work without asking for passphrase the connection must be added without specifying `--identity`.

I.e., this connection will ask for passphrase every time:
`podman system connection add --identity ~/.ssh/id_ecdsa remote_srv ssh://<remote>/run/podman/podman.sock`

This will use ssh-agent:
`podman system connection add remote_srv ssh://<remote>/run/podman/podman.sock`

I am also running into this problem that `podman --remote` is not respecting the ssh-agent. I am setting up the connection like @dmisc described, without specifying an identity file:

```bash
podman-remote system connection add remote_srv ssh://host/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock

podman system connection list

Name        URI                                                           Identity    Default     ReadWrite
remote_srv  ssh://host:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock              true        true

```

```bash
podman-remote --log-level=debug -c remote_srv info
INFO[0000] podman-remote filtering at log level debug   
DEBU[0000] Called info.PersistentPreRunE(podman-remote --log-level=debug -c remote_srv info) 
DEBU[0000] ssh_config alias found: host  
DEBU[0000]   User: imgbuilder                           
DEBU[0000]   Hostname: ssh://host:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 
DEBU[0000]   Port: 22                                   
DEBU[0000]   IdentityFile: "/home/User/.ssh/imgbuild_ed25519" 
Key Passphrase:
```

SSH-config entry:

```
Host host
   User imgbuilder
   IdentityFile ~/.ssh/imgbuild_ed25519
   IdentitiesOnly yes
   StrictHostKeyChecking no
```

And each time as seen above, podman will ask for the password, ignoring that the key is already available in the agent, since its never checked:

```bash
ssh-add -l

... there are more keys here that I am omitting ...
256 SHA256:VgvVeUcK0sw+VfdlTsw0L5Ow7UT7JFYXbXko69iiI9U imgbuild@host (ED25519)

```

The problem is that even if no identity is configured when adding a connection, it will explicitly set one when finding a suitable `IdentityFile` entry in the ssh-config, as can be seen from the debug output above. But then, the `ValidateAndConfigure` function, which includes the snippet that @dmisc posted above, will never be called with a blank identity, thus never reaching the `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` check. So it always requires the user to re-enter the passphrase if there is any.

Ideally, I think the `ValidateAndConfigure` function should check for the existence of an ssh-agent first, and only fallback to reading the key-file directly when there is no Agent, or if the key wasn't found in it.

The Code responsible for finding the correct alias in the ssh-config which also sets up the identity path is this:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/c6077f645788743258a1a749f8005b4fb3cbe533/pkg/bindings/connection.go#L240-L272

The above is fine, but the checks below should be swapped, or rather the `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` check should come first, as `iden` will never be blank with a ssh-config that contains an `IdentityFile`:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/189b09d82e7c7892b8dba5c154e0210550b3fd55/vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/ssh/connection_golang.go#L224-L232

I am also having the same issue.

Architecturally, it feels strange to split these checks between the caller (podman) and the called (common). 

It would also be nice to allow "use ssh-agent signer" to be specified in the cli or config somewhere.

Ran into this as well but what was odd was that it did work a few times even with `IdentityFile` defined globally in the `ssh_config` . Without knowing exactly what I changed (if anything) tho, I suspect that there might have been something overwriting the identity file making it empty.
